key_insights.insight: Sustainable change is made carefully with reflection and small actions, rather than drastic or quick measures.
key_insights.quote: Big meaningful change is not made uh drastically and quickly you know sustainable change is made carefully and there's this processof it's not just action there is a lot of kind of reflection and then there's a bit of action then there's a bit more reflection of like we tried that how was it you know do we need to change direction you know we keep moving so it's a kind of bit by bit by bit.

key_insights.insight: It's impossible to stop caring about what people think; the key is managing your reaction to those thoughts.
key_insights.quote: And that is impossible because you're built to care what people think of you.And you probably wouldn't function in a society that well if you didn't care what anybodythought of you.It's about how you then manage it.

key_insights.insight: Rejection is especially difficult when it triggers a core belief of being unlovable, leading to psychological distress.
key_insights.quote: Certainly if rejection taps into what we call a kind of core belief. So if someone grew up with a core belief around being unlovable, for example...What that does is when there's signs that you're not going to keep up with those rules for living, you then, it kind of triggers that core belief to come to the forefront. And that's when you get that rush of kind of psychological distress.

key_insights.insight: Emotions are not to be judged but seen as information; they are your brain's best guess at what's happening, and it's your job to work out if it's right or wrong.
key_insights.quote: Emotions get such a bad rap don't they...actually the emotion isn't the thing to judge the emotionis information it's your brain's best guess at what might be going on around youand your brain sometimes gets it right and sometimes gets it wrong and it's your job towork that out.

key_insights.insight: Confidence grows by being willing to be without it; you must be willing to feel vulnerable to expand your comfort zone.
key_insights.quote: If you're only ever willing to be in the situations where you feel confident, then it can't grow... If you're not willing to be without that confident feeling...then your confidence can't grow.

key_insights.insight: For many, connecting with others is far more important than career or money.
key_insights.quote: When it comes to a happy life, relationships beat money, fame, social class, and all the things we're told to put F into.

key_insights.insight: Values evolve over time so value check-ins are important to do at various points in your life.
key_insights.quote: I talk about doing sort of quite regular values check-ins because depending on your life you know stage and what you're doing your circumstances your values will change

key_insights.insight: We are subjected to ideals that we are trying to do everything perfectly, this is impossible.
key_insights.quote: We're subjected to these kind of ideals.We're trying to do everything perfectly and it's impossible.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice box breathing to quickly reduce anxiety.
actionable_advice.how_to: Visualize a box. Breathe in for 4 seconds while tracing one side, hold for 4 seconds tracing the next, breathe out for 4 seconds tracing the third, and hold again for 4 seconds tracing the last side.

actionable_advice.advice: If you can't afford therapy, journal and reflect on patterns of behavior with a trusted friend.
actionable_advice.how_to: Write down what happened, how you felt, how the other person might have felt, what happened before and after, looking for recurring themes. Discuss with a friend for a new perspective.

actionable_advice.advice: Determine your values and assess how well you're living in line with them in all aspects of your life.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify key areas of your life (e.g., relationships, career, health). Write down the kind of person you want to be in each area. Rate how well you're living up to that ideal, and identify discrepancies to address.

memorable_stories: Dr. Smith's story of reluctantly joining TikTok and its overwhelming positive response, leading to a large following.
The host's experience of only remembering the single negative comment from hundreds of positive ones.
The host's past experience being in a relationship that broke up because it had issues, due to the social pressure of relationships being perceived as perfect
contrarian_claims: It is impossible to 'stop caring what everybody thinks' because we are built to care.
High self-esteem isn't always useful because in situations where you're not doing great, you have to be honest with yourself.
Affirmations can be detrimental if you don't believe them.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World Leading Psychologist: How To Detach From Overthinking & Anxiety: Dr Julie Smith
